title: Kortex v2 frontmatter
description: Canonical schema for Kortex v2 markdown file frontmatter. Consolidates v1 frontmatter.md + idea-frontmatter.md + the schema content embedded in v1 root CLAUDE.md into a single source of truth. v1 .md rules files are obsolete after v2 — this JSON Schema is the authority.

title.description: Human-readable title. Required on every indexable .md file.

type.description: Type discriminator. v1 had drift with ~42 ad-hoc values across the corpus; v2 enforces 14 only. See schema/types.md for the canonical reference of each value.

layer.description: Conceptual layer in the five-zone architecture. v1 mixed `capa/N-...` tags with `layer:` field — v2 keeps only `layer:` and removes the `capa/*` tag convention entirely. `source` = raw imported material, `synthesis` = wiki layer, `project` = active execution, `schema` = framework/operational.

language.description: Content language. `en-es` indicates intentional bilingual content.

tags.description: Lowercase kebab-case tags. Forward-slash hierarchical tags allowed (e.g. `area/finance`). The v1 `capa/*` convention is REMOVED — `layer:` field replaces it.

updated.description: ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) of last meaningful edit. Bump on substantive changes; whitespace edits do not require a bump.

status.description: Lifecycle status. The first three (`active|draft|archived`) apply to any content type. The remaining values are pipeline states for `type: idea` items. A non-idea page using a pipeline state is allowed but unusual.

distillation_level.description: Distillation maturity. 0=raw dump, 1=first pass, 2=distilled, 3=synthesized, 4=expression-ready. Required on wiki pages by convention; the lint command surfaces wiki pages missing this field as distillation debt.

confidence.description: Trust level of the claims on the page. Orthogonal to `distillation_level` — distillation tracks maturity, confidence tracks reliability. A level-3 page can be confidence-low if its sources are weak.

source_count.description: Number of independent sources backing the page's claims.

last_verified.description: Date the claims were last cross-checked against external sources.

supersedes.description: Relative path to an older page that this page replaces. Pair with `superseded_by` on the older page to make the chain bidirectional.

superseded_by.description: Relative path to a newer page that replaces this one. Prevents silent contradictions sitting in the wiki.

related_paths.description: Cross-references as relative paths from the source file's directory. Replaces v1's deprecated `related:` (Obsidian wikilinks) field.

course.description: Course slug — when the page belongs to a specific course in `sources/courses/`.

project.description: Project slug — when the page belongs to a specific project under `projects/`.

angle.description: One-sentence editorial angle. What makes this idea distinct from N similar pieces on the same topic. Required on `type: idea`.

target_channel.description: Destination project. The ingest command routes by THIS field, not by the inbox path. An idea dropped in `/inbox/` with `target_channel: milo-ia` is a Milo IA idea regardless of physical location. Use `cross-project` when the destination is genuinely ambiguous at capture time.

why_it_works.description: Why the angle engages the target audience. Distinguishes a strong angle from variants of the same surface idea.

created_in.description: Surface where the idea was captured. Feeds the 'where am I most productive?' metric.

created_date.description: Capture date — distinct from `updated`, which tracks last edit.

round.description: Round identifier when the idea came from an ideation round (e.g. `2026-04-20-ronda-01`).

score_initial.description: Initial score from the scoring pass (typically /50).

plan.description: Plan tier required to execute the idea (e.g. `Free`, `Pro`, `Max`).

prompt_version.description: Version of the prompt-master that generated the idea.
